lines.translation: リサはここ数日、何か考え事をしているようだった。昨日、林雪梅が何度か尋ねて、ようやく張天明と喧嘩したことを話した。
雪梅が初めてリサに会った時、リサは張天明とは高校の同級生だと言っていた。天明はいい人で、とても社交的で、成績も優秀だ。趣味の面では、彼女と天明はあまり似ていない。天明はサッカーファンで、テレビで試合があれば必ず見る。リサは音楽ファンで、コンサートがあれば聞きに行く。リサは、趣味が違っても、二人はずっと仲良くやってきたと言っていた。
林雪梅はあれこれ考えたが、二人の間に一体何があったのか分からなかった。文化的な背景が違うからだろうか？それとも天明に新しい彼女ができたのだろうか？ちょうど今晩は暇だったので、雪梅はリサに会いに行った。
もういいよ。彼の心には私なんていないのよ。
2週間前、彼とコンサートに行く約束をしたの。まさか彼が夜通しパソコンで遊んで、コンサートのことをすっかり忘れるなんて思わなかったわ。これで怒らないわけないでしょ？
怒らないで。ケリンもよくこうなのよ。サッカーの試合を見始めると、他のことは全部忘れちゃうの。
もっと頭に来ることがあるの。先週の土曜日、彼を映画に誘ったの。彼は喜んで承諾したわ。でも8時の映画なのに、8時15分まで待っても彼が来なかったの。携帯に電話したら、別の映画館に行っていたことが分かったのよ。どれも些細なことだけど、本当に腹が立つわ。彼は私のことを全く気にかけていないように感じるの。本当に彼と別れたい。
ああ、そういうことだったのね。この天明ったら、なんてうっかりしてるの！その後どうなったの？
後で彼が私に会った時、ごめんなさいって何度も謝ってきたの。彼の態度はすごく良かったわ。それで、やっぱり彼の心の中には私がいるんだって感じたの。
うん、怒らないで！うっかりしているのと、あなたのことが心にないのとは全く違うわよ。もしある日、二人が喧嘩しなくなったら、もしかしたらあなたは…
…本当に別れちゃうかもね。実は、ケリンも天明と大して変わらないのよ。いつもあれこれ忘れっぽくて、本当にうっかり屋なの。ある時は私の誕生日を忘れ、またある時は鍵が見つからず、そしてまた…時々、本当にイライラするわ。
本当？じゃあ、ケリンもうっかりすることがあるのね。ところで、家族はあなたに彼氏がいることを知ってるの？
うーん…まだ家族にはケリンとのことを話していないの。

language_notes.label: a
language_notes.explanation_en: 中国語の口語では、「什么」は疑問代名詞としてではなく、不定の指示語として使われることがあります。この場合、文の意味に影響を与えることなく省略できます。

language_notes.label: b
language_notes.explanation_en: 「好」は軽い強調として使われることがあります。例えば、「好几个人」は「かなり多くの人」という意味です。ここで「几」は疑問代名詞ではなく、「いくつか」という少数の不定の数を表します。

language_notes.label: c
language_notes.explanation_en: 「同」は「同じ」や「似ている」という意味の形容詞です。通常、単独で使われることはありません。

language_notes.label: d
language_notes.explanation_en: ここで「提」は「言及する」という意味です。誰かが機嫌が悪い時に「别提了」と言う場合、話し手は相手にその話題をやめるように求めているというよりは、その話題に対する自分の感情的な反応を示しています。

language_notes.label: e
language_notes.explanation_en: 「叫」と「让」は、「誰かに何かをさせる」という意味で使われる場合、交換可能です。

language_notes.label: f
language_notes.explanation_en: ここで「跑」は口語で「行く」という意味で使われます。

language_notes.label: g
language_notes.explanation_en: 中国北部では、「这么」は通常「zhème」ではなく「zème」と発音されます。

language_notes.label: h
language_notes.explanation_en: 「忘这忘那」という表現では、「这」（これ）と「那」（あれ）はどちらも不定の指示語として使われます。これは日本語の「あれこれ」と同じような使い方です。

grammar_sections.title: (在) …上
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 「(在)…上」は抽象名詞と組み合わせて「〜の点で」という意味になります。例えば、性格、興味、勉強、仕事などです。

examples.hanzi: 在兴趣上, 丽莎跟天明不太一样。
examples.pinyin: Zài xìngqù shàng, Lìshā gēn Tiānmíng bú tài yíyàng.
examples.translation: 趣味の点では、リーシャとティエンミンはあまり同じではありません。

examples.hanzi: 小林最近在学习上有很多问题, 所以不太高兴。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo Lín zuìjìn zài xuéxí shàng yǒu hěn duō wèntí, suǒyǐ bú tài gāoxìng.
examples.translation: リン君は最近、勉強でたくさんの問題を抱えているので、あまり元気がないです。

examples.hanzi: 在生活上, 如果有什么问题, 就告诉我。
examples.pinyin: Zài shēnghuó shàng, rúguǒ yǒu shénme wèntí, jiù gàosu wǒ.
examples.translation: 日常生活で何か困ったことがあったら、教えてください。

grammar_sections.title: 動詞 来 動詞 去
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 「動詞 来 動詞 去」という構文は、反復的な動作を表します。例えば、走来走去 [zh:zǒuláizǒuqù] (行ったり来たり歩く)、飞来飞去 [zh:fēiláifēiqù] (あちこち飛び回る)、想来想去 [zh:xiǎngláixiǎngqù] (何度も考える)、说来说去 [zh:shuōláishuōqù] (繰り返し言う)、讨论来讨论去 [zh:tǎolùnláitǎolùnqù] (何度も議論する)、研究来研究去 [zh:yánjiūláiyánjiūqù] (何度も検討する・研究する) などです。

patterns.meaning_en: 繰り返しの動作を表します。

examples.hanzi: 你别在房间里走来走去，大家都睡觉了。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ bié zài fángjiān li zǒu lái zǒu qù, dàjiā dōu shuìjiào le.
examples.translation: 部屋の中を行ったり来たりしないでください。みんな寝ていますから。

examples.hanzi: 这个问题我们讨论来讨论去，最后还是没有办法解决。
examples.pinyin: Zhè ge wèntí wǒmen tǎolùn lái tǎolùn qù, zuìhòu háishi méiyǒu bànfǎ jiějué.
examples.translation: 私たちはこの問題を何度も議論しました。結局、解決策を見つけることができませんでした。

examples.hanzi: 妈妈叫我学经济，我想来想去还是选了化学。
examples.pinyin: Māma jiào wǒ xué jīngjì, wǒ xiǎng lái xiǎng qù háishi xuǎn le huàxué.
examples.translation: 母は私に経済学を勉強してほしがっていました。散々考えた結果、私は化学を勉強することに決めました。

grammar_sections.title: 的、得、地の比較 (2)
grammar_sections.explanation_en:
これら3つの構造助詞は発音は同じですが、使い方が異なります。

的は、(A)、(B)、(C) のように、連体修飾語と名詞をつなぎます。

得は、(D)、(E)、(F) のように、動詞や形容詞と様態補語をつなぎます。

地は、(G)、(H) のように、副詞と動詞をつなぎます。

examples.hanzi: 请帮我买点新鲜的青菜水果。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng bāng wǒ mǎi diǎn xīnxiān de qīngcài shuǐguǒ.
examples.translation: 新鮮な野菜と果物を少し買ってきてください。

examples.hanzi: 妈妈给我买的衣服是纯棉的。
examples.pinyin: Māma gěi wǒ mǎi de yīfu shì chúnmián de.
examples.translation: 母が私に買ってくれた服は綿100%です。

examples.hanzi: 你说的那位教授我不认识。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ shuō de nà wèi jiàoshòu wǒ bú rènshi.
examples.translation: あなたが話している教授のことは知りません。

examples.hanzi: 教授说我选课选得很好。
examples.pinyin: Jiàoshòu shuō wǒ xuǎn kè xuǎn de hěn hǎo.
examples.translation: 私の教授は、私の履修選択が良かったと言いました。

examples.hanzi: 我是个不怕热的人。今天热得连我都受不了了。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ shì ge bú pà rè de rén. Jīntiān rè de lián wǒ dōu shòu bu liǎo le.
examples.translation: 私は暑がりではありませんが、今日はあまりにも暑くて、私でさえ耐えられません。

examples.hanzi: 孩子们玩儿电脑玩儿得忘了吃饭。
examples.pinyin: Háizimen wánr diànnǎo wánr de wàng le chī fàn.
examples.translation: 子供たちはコンピューターゲームに夢中になりすぎて、食事を忘れてしまいました。

examples.hanzi: 女儿的病还不好,王太太着急地给医生打了一个电话。
examples.pinyin: Nǚ'ér de bìng hái bù hǎo, Wáng tàitai zháojí de gěi yīshēng dǎ le yí ge diànhuà.
examples.translation: 王さんの娘さんの病気がなかなか良くならず、王さんは心配して医者に電話しました。

examples.hanzi: 看完电视,爷爷奶奶慢慢地走上楼去休息。
examples.pinyin: Kàn wán diànshì, yéye nǎinai mànmàn de zǒu shàng lóu qù xiūxi.
examples.translation: テレビを見た後、おばあさんとおじいさんはゆっくりと二階へ休みに行きました。

grammar_sections.title: 原来 [zh:yuánlái]：副詞と形容詞
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 原来 [zh:yuánlái] には2つの意味があります。1つ目は、(A)、(B)、(C) のように、節の冒頭で突然の気づきを表すのに使われます。2つ目は、「以前は、変化が起こる前は」という意味で、(D)、(E) のように名詞の前に形容詞として、または (F)、(G)、(H) のように動詞の前に副詞として使われます。形容詞として使う場合、原来 [zh:yuánlái] の後には必ず的 [zh:de] が続くことに注意してください。

patterns.meaning_en: 節の冒頭で使われ、突然の気づき、「〜だったのか」というニュアンスを表します。
形容詞として、「元の」または「以前の」という意味です。
副詞として、「以前は」または「元々は」という意味です。

examples.hanzi: 我早就听说有一个新同屋要来，原来就是你呀。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zǎojiù tīngshuō yǒu yí ge xīn tóngwū yào lái, yuánlái jiùshì nǐ ya.
examples.translation: 新しいルームメイトが来ると聞いていました。あなただったんですね！

examples.hanzi: 房间里热得很，原来窗户没开。
examples.pinyin: Fángjiān li rè de hěn, yuánlái chuānghu méi kāi.
examples.translation: 部屋がとても暑かったです。窓が開いていなかったんですね。

examples.hanzi: 我觉得好像在哪儿见过你，原来你是我的同学的姐姐。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ juéde hǎoxiàng zài nǎr jiàn guo nǐ, yuánlái nǐ shì wǒ de tóngxué de jiějie.
examples.translation: どこかであなたを見たことがあると思っていました。あなたは私のクラスメートのお姉さんだったんですね。

examples.hanzi: 你还住在原来的宿舍吗？
examples.pinyin: Nǐ hái zhù zài yuánlái de sùshè ma?
examples.translation: 以前住んでいたのと同じ寮にまだ住んでいますか？

examples.hanzi: 这栋楼还是原来的样子，又小又旧。
examples.pinyin: Zhè dòng lóu háishi yuánlái de yàngzi, yòu xiǎo yòu jiù.
examples.translation: この建物は以前と変わっていません。小さくて古いままです。

examples.hanzi: 他原来住在学校的宿舍里，后来搬到校外去了。
examples.pinyin: Tā yuánlái zhù zài xuéxiào de sùshè li, hòulái bān dào xiàowài qù le.
examples.translation: 彼は以前、学内の寮に住んでいましたが、その後、学外に引っ越しました。

examples.hanzi: 她原来吃肉，现在吃起素来了。
examples.pinyin: Tā yuánlái chī ròu, xiànzài chī qǐ sù lai le.
examples.translation: 彼女は以前、肉を食べていましたが、今はベジタリアンです。

examples.hanzi: 我的同屋原来不喜欢吃菠菜，后来听说菠菜对身体健康有好处，就开始吃菠菜了。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de tóngwū yuánlái bù xǐhuan chī bōcài, hòulái tīngshuō bōcài duì shēntǐ jiànkāng yǒu hǎochu, jiù kāishǐ chī bōcài le.
examples.translation: 私のルームメイトは、もともとほうれん草が好きではありませんでした。その後、ほうれん草が健康に良いと聞いて、食べるようになりました。

usage_notes: 形容詞として使う場合、[zh:原来] の後には [zh:的] が必要です。

grammar_sections.title: 成語
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 中国語には多くの成語があります。その形は固定されていることが多く、文字通りの意味で捉えるべきではありません。つまり、全体の意味は個々の単語の意味の合計ではないため、表現全体を覚えるのが一番です。これらの成語は通常四文字で構成されており、このレッスンに出てくる[zh:一干二净]や[zh:丢三拉四]のように、多くは慣用句です。成語は単語のように機能しますが、文法的な文脈が限られていることが多いです。例えば、[zh:一干二净]は形容詞的ですが、補語として使われることが最も多いです。[zh:丢三拉四]は動詞のように機能し、述語として使うことができますが、目的語や補語を持つことはできません。

patterns.meaning_en: すっかり、完全に、きれいさっぱり
そそっかしい、忘れっぽい

examples.hanzi: 两个星期前我跟他约好去看一个演唱会, 没想到他那天晚上一直玩儿电脑, 把演唱会的事儿忘得一干二净。
examples.pinyin: Liǎng ge xīngqī qián wǒ gēn tā yuē hǎo qù kàn yí ge yǎnchànghuì, méi xiǎng dào tā nà tiān wǎnshang yìzhí wánr diànnǎo, bǎ yǎnchànghuì de shìr wàng de yì gān èr jìng.
examples.translation: 2週間前、彼とコンサートに行く約束をしました。まさか彼がその夜、コンピューターゲームをしてコンサートのことをすっかり忘れてしまうとは思いませんでした。

examples.hanzi: 上个学期学的汉字, 他已经忘得一干二净了。
examples.pinyin: Shàng ge xuéqī xué de Hànzì, tā yǐjīng wàng de yì gān èr jìng le.
examples.translation: 彼は去年の学期に習った漢字を一つ残らず忘れてしまいました。

examples.hanzi: 他请我们去饭馆儿吃饭, 我去晚了一会儿, 到那儿的时候, 他们已经把菜吃得一干二净了。
examples.pinyin: Tā qǐng wǒmen qù fànguǎnr chīfàn, wǒ qù wǎn le yíhuìr, dào nàr de shíhou, tāmen yǐjīng bǎ cài chī de yì gān èr jìng le.
examples.translation: 彼が私たちを夕食に招待してくれました。私が少し遅れて着くと、彼らはもう全部食べてしまっていました。

examples.hanzi: 他把房间打扫得一干二净。
examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ fángjiān dǎsǎo de yì gān èr jìng.
examples.translation: 彼は部屋をピカピカにしました。

examples.hanzi: 实际上, 柯林跟天明差不多, 常常忘这忘那、丢三拉四的。
examples.pinyin: Shíjìshang, Kē Lín gēn Tiānmíng chàbuduō, chángcháng wàng zhè wàng nà, diū sān là sì de.
examples.translation: 実は、柯林は天明とよく似ていて、いつもあれこれ忘れっぽく、そそっかしいです。

examples.hanzi: 我哥哥常常丢三拉四的, 我看有一天他会把自己也丢了。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ gēge chángcháng diū sān là sì de, wǒ kàn yǒu yì tiān tā huì bǎ zìjǐ yě diū le.
examples.translation: うちの兄はとてもそそっかしいので、いつか自分自身をなくしても驚きません。

examples.hanzi: 你再这么丢三拉四的, 你女朋友非跟你分手不可。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ zài zhème diū sān là sì de, nǐ nǚpéngyou fēi gēn nǐ fēnshǒu bùkě.
examples.translation: そんなに忘れっぽくてうっかりしていると、遅かれ早かれ彼女に振られてしまいますよ。

usage_notes: 成語は形が固定されており、文字通りの意味で捉えるべきではありません。
全体の意味は個々の単語の意味の合計ではないため、表現全体を覚えるのが一番です。
通常四文字で構成されており、多くは慣用句です。
成語は単語のように機能しますが、文法的な文脈が限られていることが多いです。
[zh:一干二净]は形容詞的ですが、補語として使われることが最も多いです。
[zh:丢三拉四]は動詞のように機能し、述語として使うことができますが、目的語や補語を持つことはできません。

translation: Lisa seems to have something on her mind these past few days. Yesterday, Lin Xuemei asked her several times, and only then did she say that she had quarreled with Zhang Tianming.

scenes.scene_goal: 展现丽莎心事重重的状态，以及朋友林雪梅的关心。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头首先对准丽莎，她坐在宿舍窗边，心不在焉地看着窗外，神情忧郁。然后镜头切换，林雪梅在旁边几次想开口问她，最后丽莎才低声说她和男友吵架了。
scenes.setting_en: A cozy and slightly messy female university dorm room, afternoon light streaming through the window.
scenes.camera_movement: Slow zoom in on Lisa, then a cut to a two-shot with Xuemei.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic, slice-of-life. A young Chinese woman (Lisa) sits by a dorm room window, looking sad and lost in thought. Her friend (Lin Xuemei) approaches her with a concerned expression, gently touches her shoulder. Lisa finally looks at her and speaks softly, looking distressed.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 丽莎这几天好像有什么心事。昨天林雪梅问了她好几次,她才说她跟张天明吵架了。
voice_over.pinyin: Lìshā zhè jǐ tiān hǎoxiàng yǒu shénme xīnshì zuótiān Lín Xuěméi wèn le tā hǎojǐ cì tā cái shuō tā gēn Zhāng Tiānmíng chǎojià le
voice_over.translation: Lisa seems to have something on her mind these past few days. Yesterday, Lin Xuemei asked her several times, and only then did she say that she had quarreled with Zhang Tianming.

hanzi: 雪梅刚认识丽莎的时候,听丽莎说,她和张天明在高中就是同学。天明人很好,很开朗,学习也不错。在兴趣上她跟天明不太一样,天明是个球迷,电视里一有球赛,他就非看不可,丽莎是个歌迷,一有演唱会就去听。丽莎说,他们的兴趣爱好虽然不同,但是一直相处得很好。
pinyin: Xuěméi gāng rènshi Lìshā de shíhou tīng Lìshā shuō tā hé Zhāng Tiānmíng zài gāozhōng jiù shì tóngxué Tiānmíng rén hěn hǎo hěn kāilǎng xuéxí yě búcuò zài xìngqù shàng tā gēn Tiānmíng bù tài yíyàng Tiānmíng shì ge qiúmí diànshì lǐ yī yǒu qiúsài tā jiù fēi kàn bùkě Lìshā shì ge gēmí yī yǒu yǎnchànghuì jiù qù tīng Lìshā shuō tāmen de xìngqù àihào suīrán bùtóng dànshì yìzhí xiāngchǔ de hěn hǎo
translation: When Xuemei first met Lisa, she heard Lisa say that she and Zhang Tianming were classmates in high school. Tianming is a nice person, very outgoing, and a good student. In terms of interests, she and Tianming are not quite the same. Tianming is a soccer fan; whenever there's a game on TV, he has to watch it. Lisa is a music fan; whenever there's a concert, she goes to listen. Lisa said that although their interests and hobbies are different, they have always gotten along well.

scenes.scene_goal: 通过闪回，介绍丽莎和张天明的背景和过去的美好关系，与现状形成对比。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 一系列快速剪辑的闪回画面：丽莎和张天明穿着高中校服在校园里笑闹；张天明在客厅沙发上激动地看球赛；丽莎戴着耳机在演唱会现场兴奋地挥舞荧光棒；两人虽然兴趣不同，但依然甜蜜地依偎在一起。画面色调温暖明快。
scenes.setting_en: Various locations in flashback: a high school campus, a living room with a TV, a concert venue.
scenes.shot_type: Various shots: Medium shot, Close-up on faces
scenes.video_prompt_en: A montage of happy memories in a warm, dreamy filter. A young couple (Lisa and Zhang Tianming) in high school uniforms laughing. The man (Tianming) excitedly watching a soccer game on TV. The woman (Lisa) at a concert, singing along happily. Despite different interests, they are shown affectionately together, smiling.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 雪梅刚认识丽莎的时候,听丽莎说,她和张天明在高中就是同学。天明人很好,很开朗,学习也不错。在兴趣上她跟天明不太一样,天明是个球迷,电视里一有球赛,他就非看不可,丽莎是个歌迷,一有演唱会就去听。丽莎说,他们的兴趣爱好虽然不同,但是一直相处得很好。
voice_over.pinyin: Xuěméi gāng rènshi Lìshā de shíhou tīng Lìshā shuō tā hé Zhāng Tiānmíng zài gāozhōng jiù shì tóngxué... tāmen de xìngqù àihào suīrán bùtóng dànshì yìzhí xiāngchǔ de hěn hǎo
voice_over.translation: When Xuemei first met Lisa, she heard Lisa say that she and Zhang Tianming were classmates in high school... Lisa said that although their interests and hobbies are different, they have always gotten along well.

hanzi: 林雪梅想来想去,想不出他们到底发生了什么事儿。是因为文化背景不同吗?还是天明有了新的女朋友?正好今天晚上有空,雪梅就去找丽莎。
pinyin: Lín Xuěméi xiǎng lái xiǎng qù xiǎng bu chū tāmen dàodǐ fāshēng le shénme shìr shì yīnwèi wénhuà bèijǐng bùtóng ma háishi Tiānmíng yǒu le xīn de nǚpéngyou zhènghǎo jīntiān wǎnshang yǒu kòng Xuěméi jiù qù zhǎo Lìshā
translation: Lin Xuemei thought it over and over, but couldn't figure out what on earth had happened between them. Was it because of their different cultural backgrounds? Or did Tianming have a new girlfriend? It just so happened that she was free this evening, so Xuemei went to see Lisa.

scenes.scene_goal: 表现林雪梅的困惑和担忧，并推动她去找丽莎的行动。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 林雪梅独自在宿舍里，一边踱步一边思考，脸上写满了困惑。她看着手机，似乎在犹豫。窗外天色渐晚。最后，她下定决心，穿上外套，开门走了出去。
scenes.setting_en: Lin Xuemei's dorm room, evening. The room is tidy.
scenes.camera_movement: Camera follows Xuemei as she paces. Close-up on her thoughtful face.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic. A concerned young woman (Lin Xuemei) paces in her dorm room in the evening, deep in thought. She looks at her phone, then at the door. With a determined expression, she grabs her jacket and leaves the room.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 林雪梅想来想去,想不出他们到底发生了什么事儿。正好今天晚上有空,雪梅就去找丽莎。
voice_over.pinyin: Lín Xuěméi xiǎng lái xiǎng qù, xiǎng bu chū tāmen dàodǐ fāshēng le shénme shìr. zhènghǎo jīntiān wǎnshang yǒu kòng, Xuěméi jiù qù zhǎo Lìshā
voice_over.translation: Lin Xuemei thought it over and over, but couldn't figure out what on earth had happened between them. It just so happened that she was free this evening, so Xuemei went to see Lisa.

hanzi: 别提了,他心里根本没有我。
两个星期前我跟他约好去看一个演唱会,没想到他那天晚上一直玩儿电脑,把演唱会的事儿忘得一干二净。你说我能不生气吗?
pinyin: bié tí le tā xīnli gēnběn méiyǒu wǒ
dào dǐ shì zěn me le

liǎng ge xīng qī qián wǒ gēn tā yuē hǎo qù kàn yí gè yǎn chàng huì méi xiǎng dào tā nà tiān wǎn shang yì zhí wánr diàn nǎo bǎ yǎn chàng huì de shìr wàng de yì gān èr jìng nǐ shuō wǒ néng bù shēng qì ma
translation: What's wrong between you and Tianming?
Don't mention it. I'm not in his heart at all.
Two weeks ago, I arranged with him to go to a concert. I didn't expect that he would be playing on his computer all night and completely forget about the concert. Tell me, how could I not be angry?

scenes.scene_goal: 展开丽莎和雪梅的对话，揭示吵架的具体原因。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 雪梅来到丽莎的宿舍，丽莎正坐在床上，看起来很沮丧。雪梅坐到她身边，关切地问她。丽莎一开始不想说，但在雪梅的追问下，终于激动地讲出了张天明忘记演唱会约定的事情。讲到激动处，丽莎眼眶泛红。
scenes.setting_en: Lisa's dorm room, evening. The atmosphere is tense and emotional.
scenes.camera_movement: Starts with a wide shot of Xuemei entering, then cuts to a two-shot of them on the bed. Close-ups on their faces during emotional dialogue.
scenes.shot_type: Wide shot, Medium two-shot, Close-up, Reaction shot
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic, evening. Lin Xuemei enters Lisa's dorm room. Lisa is sitting on her bed, looking dejected. Xuemei sits beside her, asking what's wrong. Lisa initially dismisses it, but then tearfully and angrily explains how her boyfriend forgot their concert date. Quick flashback of him engrossed in his computer while she waits with two tickets.

hanzi: 是啊,别生气了!马虎跟心里没有你根本不同。如果有一天不吵了,说不定你们就
pinyin: shì a bié shēngqì le mǎhu gēn xīnli méiyǒu nǐ gēnběn bùtóng rúguǒ yǒu yì tiān bù chǎo le shuōbudìng nǐmen jiù
translation: Yeah, don't be angry! Being careless is completely different from not having you in his heart. If one day you two stop fighting, maybe you will...

scenes.scene_goal: 林雪梅安慰丽莎，指出“马虎”和“不在乎”的区别，并留下悬念。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头聚焦于林雪梅。她真诚地看着丽莎，轻轻摇头，试图让她宽心。当她说到“马虎”和“心里没有你”时，她用手势做了个区分的动作。最后，她带着一丝调侃的微笑，话说到一半停了下来，给丽莎留下想象空间。
scenes.setting_en: Cozy, warm-lit cafe interior. Afternoon light streams through the window.
scenes.camera_movement: Slow push-in on Lin Xuemei's face.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium close-up shot of a gentle young East Asian woman (Lin Xuemei) in a cozy cafe, leaning forward and speaking earnestly to her friend. She makes a subtle hand gesture to differentiate two points, then smiles knowingly, trailing off her sentence. Soft, warm lighting, cinematic.

voice_over.speaker: 林雪梅
voice_over.hanzi: 是啊,别生气了!马虎跟心里没有你根本不同。如果有一天不吵了,说不定你们就
voice_over.pinyin: shì a bié shēngqì le mǎhu gēn xīnli méiyǒu nǐ gēnběn bùtóng rúguǒ yǒu yì tiān bù chǎo le shuōbudìng nǐmen jiù
voice_over.translation: Yeah, don't be angry! Being careless is completely different from not having you in his heart. If one day you two stop fighting, maybe you will...

hanzi: 真的会分手。实际上,柯林跟天明差不多,常常忘这忘那、丢三拉四的。他一会儿忘了我的生日,一会儿找不到钥匙,一会儿又……………有的时候真让人着急。
pinyin: zhēn de huì fēn shǒu shí jì shang kē lín gēn tiān míng chà bu duō cháng cháng wàng zhè wàng nà diū sān lā sì de tā yī huìr wàng le wǒ de shēng rì yī huìr zhǎo bú dào yào shi yī huìr yòu yǒu de shí hou zhēn ràng rén zhāo jí
translation: ...really break up. Actually, Ke Lin is not much different from Tianming. He's always forgetting this and that, very scatterbrained. One minute he forgets my birthday, the next he can't find his keys, and then again... Sometimes it's really frustrating.

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎打断林雪梅，转而抱怨自己的男朋友柯林也同样“丢三拉四”，并用闪回画面表现他的马虎行为。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 丽莎接着林雪梅的话，自嘲地笑了笑。随着她的讲述，画面中快速穿插几个风格化的黑白闪回：柯林站在一个蛋糕前，表情迷茫，显然忘了是谁的生日；柯林在门口焦急地翻着背包找钥匙。最后镜头切回咖啡馆，丽莎无奈地摊开手，表情又好气又好笑。
scenes.setting_en: Cozy cafe interior, with brief, stylized black-and-white flashback inserts.
scenes.camera_movement: Quick cuts between Lisa in the cafe and the flashback scenes.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Close-up on a young East Asian woman (Lisa) in a cafe, speaking with a mix of frustration and amusement. Quick cuts to comical, black-and-white flashbacks: a scatterbrained young man (Ke Lin) looking confused in front of a birthday cake; the same man frantically searching his pockets for keys. The scene ends with Lisa shrugging in exasperation.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 真的会分手。实际上,柯林跟天明差不多,常常忘这忘那、丢三拉四的。他一会儿忘了我的生日,一会儿找不到钥匙,一会儿又……………有的时候真让人着急。
voice_over.pinyin: zhēn de huì fēn shǒu shí jì shang kē lín gēn tiān míng chà bu duō cháng cháng wàng zhè wàng nà diū sān lā sì de tā yī huìr wàng le wǒ de shēng rì yī huìr zhǎo bú dào yào shi yī huìr yòu yǒu de shí hou zhēn ràng rén zhāo jí
voice_over.translation: ...really break up. Actually, Ke Lin is not much different from Tianming. He's always forgetting this and that, very scatterbrained. One minute he forgets my birthday, the next he can't find his keys, and then again... Sometimes it's really frustrating.

hanzi: 是吗?原来柯林也有马虎的时候。对了,你交男朋友的事儿,家里知道吗?
pinyin: shì ma yuán lái kē lín yě yǒu mǎ hu de shí hou duì le nǐ jiāo nán péng you de shìr jiā lǐ zhī dao ma
translation: Really? So Ke Lin can be careless too. By the way, does your family know that you have a boyfriend?

scenes.scene_goal: 林雪梅听到柯林的事后感到惊讶，随即话锋一转，八卦地询问丽莎的家人是否知情。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 林雪梅听到丽莎的抱怨，先是露出惊讶的表情，随即轻笑出声。她身体向后靠了一下，然后突然又凑近丽莎，压低声音，带着一丝狡黠和好奇的眼神，八卦地转换了话题。
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot, focusing on Lin Xuemei's changing expressions.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Two-shot in a cafe. An East Asian woman (Lin Xuemei) reacts with surprised laughter, then leans in conspiratorially towards her friend (Lisa), lowering her voice and asking a personal question with a curious, playful smile. Warm, natural lighting.

voice_over.speaker: 林雪梅
voice_over.hanzi: 是吗?原来柯林也有马虎的时候。对了,你交男朋友的事儿,家里知道吗?
voice_over.pinyin: shì ma yuán lái kē lín yě yǒu mǎ hu de shí hou duì le nǐ jiāo nán péng you de shìr jiā lǐ zhī dao ma
voice_over.translation: Really? So Ke Lin can be careless too. By the way, does your family know that you have a boyfriend?

hanzi: 这个吗………………我还没把我跟柯林的事儿告诉家里呢。
pinyin: zhè ge ma wǒ hái méi bǎ wǒ gēn kē lín de shìr gào su jiā lǐ ne

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎被问住，表情变得犹豫和为难，承认自己还没告诉家人。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头特写丽莎的脸。被林雪梅突然一问，她明显愣住了。她的眼神躲闪，避开朋友的目光，无意识地用手指敲击着咖啡杯，显得有些局促和犹豫。停顿片刻后，她才小声地、略带为难地回答。
scenes.video_prompt_en: Close-up on a young East Asian woman's (Lisa) face in a cafe. Her expression shifts to hesitation and awkwardness after being asked a question. She avoids eye contact, looking down and fidgeting slightly, before answering in a soft, reluctant voice. Cinematic, shallow depth of field.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to describe someone seeming troubled and to report a quarrel.

narration.subtitle_en: This first sentence sets the scene. It says: 'Lisa seems to have something on her mind these past few days. Yesterday, Lin Xuemei asked her several times, and only then did she say that she had quarreled with Zhang Tianming.' The key phrase here is [zh:有什么心事], which means 'to have something weighing on one's mind.' We also learn that Lisa [zh:吵架了], 'had a quarrel,' with Zhang Tianming. Notice the use of [zh:好几次], meaning 'several times,' which emphasizes how concerned Lin Xuemei was.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 丽莎这几天好像有什么心事。昨天林雪梅问了她好几次,她才说她跟张天明吵架了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lìshā zhè jǐ tiān hǎoxiàng yǒu shénme xīnshì. Zuótiān Lín Xuěméi wènle tā hǎo jǐ cì, tā cái shuō tā gēn Zhāng Tiānmíng chǎojià le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Lisa seems to have something on her mind these past few days. Yesterday, Lin Xuemei asked her several times, and only then did she say that she had quarreled with Zhang Tianming.

segments.visual_notes: Animate text highlights for '心事', '好几次', and '吵架了'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the words for 'something on one's mind' and 'to quarrel'.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's look at two key words. First, [zh:心事]. It's composed of [zh:心], meaning 'heart' or 'mind', and [zh:事], meaning 'matter' or 'affair'. Together, they create the image of a matter weighing on your heart. Second, [zh:吵架]. This is a verb-object phrase. [zh:吵] means 'to make a noise' or 'to be noisy', and [zh:架] here means 'fight' or 'row'. So, 'to make a noisy fight' is to quarrel.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 心事 / 吵架
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: xīnshì / chǎo jià
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: something on one's mind / to quarrel

highlight_words.character_insight_en: 心 (heart) + 事 (matter) = A matter on one's heart.
吵 (noisy) + 架 (fight) = A noisy fight.

segments.visual_notes: Show the character breakdown for each word.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to talk about the past and how people know each other.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, we get some background on Lisa and Tianming's relationship. The sentence means: 'When Xuemei first met Lisa, she heard Lisa say that she and Zhang Tianming were classmates in high school.' The phrase [zh:刚...的时候] means 'when...first...'. And [zh:高中] is 'senior high school'. So they've known each other for a long time.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 雪梅刚认识丽莎的时候,听丽莎说,她和张天明在高中就是同学。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xuěméi gāng rènshi Lìshā de shíhou, tīng Lìshā shuō, tā hé Zhāng Tiānmíng zài gāozhōng jiù shì tóngxué.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: When Xuemei first met Lisa, she heard Lisa say that she and Zhang Tianming were classmates in high school.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the time phrase '刚...的时候'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn adjectives for describing a person's character and abilities.

narration.subtitle_en: This part describes Tianming's personality. It says: 'Tianming is a nice person, very outgoing, and a good student.' The phrase [zh:人很好] is a very common and natural way to say someone is a 'nice person'. [zh:开朗] means 'outgoing' or 'open and sunny'. And [zh:学习也不错] means his 'studies are also not bad,' which is a modest way of saying he's a good student.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 天明人很好,很开朗,学习也不错。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tiānmíng rén hěn hǎo, hěn kāilǎng, xuéxí yě búcuò.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Tianming is a nice person, very outgoing, and a good student.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to talk about interests and use the 'in terms of' and 'must do' structures.

narration.subtitle_en: Now we see their differences. This part says: 'In terms of interests, she and Tianming are not quite the same. Tianming is a soccer fan; whenever there's a game on TV, he has to watch it.' The structure [zh:在...上] means 'in terms of...'. Tianming is a [zh:球迷], a 'ball game fan'. The structure [zh:一...就...] means 'as soon as... then...'. And [zh:非看不可] is a strong phrase meaning 'absolutely must watch'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 在兴趣上她跟天明不太一样,天明是个球迷,电视里一有球赛,他就非看不可,

on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: In terms of interests, she and Tianming are not quite the same. Tianming is a soccer fan; whenever there's a game on TV, he has to watch it,

segments.visual_notes: Animate the structure '在[兴趣]上' and '非[看]不可'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe being a fan of music and concerts.

narration.subtitle_en: In contrast to Tianming, 'Lisa is a music fan; whenever there's a concert, she goes to listen.' Just like [zh:球迷] for a sports fan, a [zh:歌迷] is a 'music fan'. The character [zh:歌] means 'song'. A [zh:演唱会] is a 'vocal concert'. We see the [zh:一...就...] structure again, showing it's a habitual action for her.

on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Lisa is a music fan; whenever there's a concert, she goes to listen.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express contrast with 'although...but...' and describe relationships.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence summarizes their relationship dynamic. 'Lisa said that although their interests and hobbies are different, they have always gotten along well.' The key structure is [zh:虽然...但是...], meaning 'although...but...'. Their [zh:兴趣爱好] 'interests and hobbies' are [zh:不同] 'different', but they [zh:一直相处得很好] 'have always gotten along very well'. The verb [zh:相处] means 'to get along'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 丽莎说,他们的兴趣爱好虽然不同,但是一直相处得很好。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lìshā shuō, tāmen de xìngqù àihào suīrán bùtóng, dànshì yìzhí xiāngchǔ de hěn hǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Lisa said that although their interests and hobbies are different, they have always gotten along well.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the '虽然...但是...' structure connecting the two clauses.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express repetitive action and speculation.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, Lin Xuemei is trying to figure out the problem. 'Lin Xuemei thought it over and over, but couldn't figure out what on earth had happened between them.' The phrase [zh:想来想去] vividly expresses the repetitive action of thinking again and again. [zh:想不出] means she 'couldn't figure it out by thinking'. [zh:到底] means 'on earth' and is used in questions to seek a definitive answer.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 林雪梅想来想去,想不出他们到底发生了什么事儿。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lín Xuěméi xiǎng lái xiǎng qù, xiǎng bu chū tāmen dàodǐ fāshēngle shénme shìr.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Lin Xuemei thought it over and over, but couldn't figure out what on earth had happened between them.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Signifies a repetitive action.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a looping arrow around the character's head for '想来想去'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to form alternative questions with 'is it... or...'.

narration.subtitle_en: Lin Xuemei continues to speculate. 'Was it because of their different cultural backgrounds? Or did Tianming have a new girlfriend?' This sentence uses the '[zh:是...还是...]' structure to pose two possible reasons. First, a different [zh:文化背景], 'cultural background'. Second, maybe he has a [zh:新的女朋友], 'new girlfriend'. This is a common way to present alternatives in a question.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 是因为文化背景不同吗?还是天明有了新的女朋友?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shì yīnwèi wénhuà bèijǐng bùtóng ma? Háishi Tiānmíng yǒule xīn de nǚpéngyou?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Was it because of their different cultural backgrounds? Or did Tianming have a new girlfriend?

segments.visual_notes: Visually separate the two possibilities on screen.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express a fortunate coincidence.

narration.subtitle_en: The narrator explains Lin Xuemei's decision. 'It just so happened that she was free this evening, so Xuemei went to see Lisa.' The word [zh:正好] means 'it just so happens' or 'coincidentally'. Because she was [zh:有空], 'had free time', she [zh:就去找丽莎], 'then went to find Lisa'. The word [zh:就] connects the reason and the resulting action.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 正好今天晚上有空,雪梅就去找丽莎。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhènghǎo jīntiān wǎnshang yǒu kòng, Xuěméi jiù qù zhǎo Lìshā.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: It just so happened that she was free this evening, so Xuemei went to see Lisa.

segments.visual_notes: Use a calendar icon with a checkmark to represent '有空'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the common phrase for asking what's wrong between people.

narration.subtitle_en: Lin Xuemei gets straight to the point. She asks, 'What's wrong between you and Tianming?' The pattern '[zh:你跟...怎么了?]' is a very direct and common way to ask someone what's wrong in their relationship with another person. It's simple but shows you've noticed something is amiss.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你跟天明怎么了?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ gēn Tiānmíng zěnme le?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What's wrong between you and Tianming?

segments.visual_notes: Show two character icons with a question mark between them.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand an emotional response and how to express feeling uncared for.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa's reply is full of emotion: 'Don't mention it. I'm not in his heart at all.' The phrase [zh:别提了] literally means 'don't mention it,' but it's used to show that the topic is upsetting. She then says [zh:他心里根本没有我], which translates to 'he simply doesn't have me in his heart.' The word [zh:根本] strongly negates the statement, meaning 'at all' or 'fundamentally'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 别提了,他心里根本没有我。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Bié tí le, tā xīnli gēnběn méiyǒu wǒ.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Don't mention it. I'm not in his heart at all.

segments.visual_notes: Show a character icon with a broken heart.

segments.teaching_goal: To master the usage of '别提了' to express frustration or sadness about a topic.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's focus on the phrase [zh:别提了]. While it literally means 'don't mention it,' it doesn't actually mean you want the other person to drop the subject. Instead, it's a way to express a strong emotional response, like frustration, sadness, or annoyance about the topic being discussed. It signals to the listener that the story you're about to tell is an unhappy one. It's like saying 'Ugh, don't even get me started.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 别提了
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: bié tí le
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Don't mention it / Don't get me started
on_screen_text.notes: Indicates an emotional response to the subject, not a request to stop talking.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An expression used to show frustration or sadness about a topic before elaborating on it.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a speech bubble with '别提了' appearing, followed by an ellipsis '...' to show more is coming.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to use '到底' to ask for the real story.

narration.subtitle_en: Lin Xuemei gently presses for more information. She asks, 'What on earth happened?' By adding [zh:到底] to the question [zh:是怎么了], she's asking for the root cause or the 'real story'. It shows she's genuinely concerned and wants to understand the situation fully. It's a bit more insistent than just asking 'What happened?'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 到底是怎么了?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dàodǐ shì zěnme le?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What on earth happened?

highlight_words.translation: what on earth, in the end

segments.visual_notes: Highlight '到底' with a magnifying glass icon.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe a past arrangement.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa starts explaining the first incident. 'Two weeks ago, I arranged with him to go to a concert.' The key phrase here is [zh:约好], which means 'to have an arrangement' or 'to have agreed upon a date'. It's more specific than just 'asked'. It implies a mutual agreement was made to go to the [zh:演唱会], the concert.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 两个星期前我跟他约好去看一个演唱会,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Liǎng ge xīngqī qián wǒ gēn tā yuē hǎo qù kàn yí ge yǎnchànghuì,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Two weeks ago, I arranged with him to go to a concert,

segments.visual_notes: Show a calendar icon with two figures and a music note to represent the plan.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express unexpected events and use the set phrase '一干二净'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's what went wrong. 'I didn't expect that he would be playing on his computer all night and completely forget about the concert.' The phrase [zh:没想到] means 'unexpectedly'. The most important part is the set phrase [zh:忘得一干二净], which means 'to forget completely and thoroughly'. It's a very strong and descriptive way to say someone forgot something.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 没想到他那天晚上一直玩儿电脑,把演唱会的事儿忘得一干二净。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: méi xiǎngdào tā nà tiān wǎnshang yìzhí wánr diànnǎo, bǎ yǎnchànghuì de shìr wàng de yì gān èr jìng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I didn't expect that he would be playing on his computer all night and completely forget about the concert.

grammar_points.explanation_en: completely, thoroughly, spotless

segments.visual_notes: Animate the concert tickets being erased or disappearing into dust.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand and use rhetorical questions to express strong feelings.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa ends her story with a rhetorical question: 'You tell me, how could I not be angry?' The structure [zh:你说我能不...吗] isn't a real question. It's a way to emphasize her strong feelings. She's not asking for permission to be angry; she's stating that her anger is completely justified. It's a powerful way to express frustration.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你说我能不生气吗?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ shuō wǒ néng bù shēngqì ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Tell me, how could I not be angry?

segments.visual_notes: Show an angry emoji or a character with steam coming out of their ears.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn phrases for comforting someone and giving a relatable example.

narration.subtitle_en: Lin Xuemei tries to comfort Lisa. 'Don't be angry. Ke Lin is often like this too. As soon as he watches a soccer game, he forgets about everything else.' She starts with [zh:别生气了], 'Don't be angry.' Then she gives an example using her own boyfriend, Ke Lin, saying he [zh:也常常这样], 'is also often like this'. The structure [zh:一看...就...] shows the immediate cause and effect: as soon as he watches a game, he forgets everything.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 别生气了,柯林也常常这样,一看球赛就什么事儿都忘了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Bié shēngqì le, Kē Lín yě chángcháng zhèyàng, yī kàn qiúsài jiù shénme shìr dōu wàng le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Don't be angry. Ke Lin is often like this too. As soon as he watches a soccer game, he forgets about everything else.

segments.visual_notes: Show a split screen: one side with an angry Lisa, the other with a distracted Ke Lin watching TV.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to introduce a more frustrating point in a story.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa is not comforted. She signals that there's more to the story by saying, 'There's something that's even more infuriating.' The phrase [zh:还有更叫人生气的呢] is a great way to transition to an even worse example. [zh:更] means 'even more', and [zh:叫人生气] means 'makes one angry'. The particle [zh:呢] at the end adds a tone of emphasis and complaint.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 还有更叫人生气的呢。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Háiyǒu gèng jiào rén shēngqì de ne.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: There's something that's even more infuriating.

segments.visual_notes: Use an arrow pointing upwards to signify 'even more' anger.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe making and agreeing to plans.

narration.subtitle_en: Here begins the second story. 'Last Saturday, I asked him to go to the movies with me. He happily agreed.' The phrase [zh:找他陪我] means 'I sought him out to accompany me'. His response was positive: [zh:他很高兴地答应了]. Notice the particle [zh:地] after the adverb [zh:很高兴], which connects the adverb to the verb [zh:答应], 'to agree'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 上个星期六我找他陪我去看电影,他很高兴地答应了,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shàng ge xīngqīliù wǒ zhǎo tā péi wǒ qù kàn diànyǐng, tā hěn gāoxìng de dāying le,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Last Saturday, I asked him to go to the movies with me. He happily agreed,

segments.visual_notes: Show two speech bubbles, one with a movie ticket icon and the other with a thumbs-up.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express a contrast and waiting for someone who is late.

narration.subtitle_en: The plan starts to go wrong. 'But for the eight o'clock movie, I waited until 8:15 and he still hadn't arrived.' The word [zh:可是] signals a turn of events. She says [zh:我等到八点一刻], 'I waited until a quarter past eight'. [zh:一刻] is a common way to say 'a quarter of an hour'. The result: [zh:他还没到], 'he still hadn't arrived'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 可是八点的电影,我等到八点一刻他还没到。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: kěshì bā diǎn de diànyǐng, wǒ děng dào bā diǎn yí kè tā hái méi dào.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: But for the eight o'clock movie, I waited until 8:15 and he still hadn't arrived.

segments.visual_notes: Show a clock with the hands moving from 8:00 to 8:15.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe finding something out and someone going to the wrong place.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa discovers the absurd reason for his lateness. 'I called his cell phone, only to find out that he had gone to another movie theater.' The phrase [zh:才知道] means 'only then did I find out'. And where was he? He had [zh:跑到另一家电影院去了]. The verb [zh:跑] here is used colloquially to mean 'to go', but it adds a sense of him rushing off in the wrong direction.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 打他手机,才知道他跑到另一家电影院去了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dǎ tā shǒujī, cái zhīdào tā pǎodào lìng yī jiā diànyǐngyuàn qù le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I called his cell phone, only to find out that he had gone to another movie theater.

segments.visual_notes: Show a map with two different movie theater locations and a confused character icon.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express that small things can lead to big feelings.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa explains why these incidents upset her so much. 'Although these are all small matters, they make me very angry. I feel like he doesn't care about me at all.' She acknowledges they are [zh:小事儿], 'small matters', using the [zh:虽然...但是...] structure. But these small things [zh:叫我非常生气], 'make me very angry', because she feels [zh:他根本不在乎我], 'he doesn't care about me at all'. [zh:在乎] means 'to care about'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这些虽然都是小事儿,但是叫我非常生气,觉得他根本不在乎我。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhèxiē suīrán dōu shì xiǎoshìr, dànshì jiào wǒ fēicháng shēngqì, juéde tā gēnběn bú zàihu wǒ.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Although these are all small matters, they make me very angry. I feel like he doesn't care about me at all.

segments.visual_notes: Show a scale, with many 'small matters' on one side weighing down the 'relationship' on the other.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the phrase for 'breaking up'.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa reaches a serious conclusion. 'I really want to break up with him.' The phrase [zh:真想] expresses a strong, genuine desire. The key vocabulary here is [zh:分手], which means 'to break up'. It's composed of [zh:分], 'to separate', and [zh:手], 'hand', literally 'to separate hands'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 真想跟他分手。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhēn xiǎng gēn tā fēnshǒu.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I really want to break up with him.

highlight_words.character_insight_en: 分 (to separate) + 手 (hand) = to separate hands.

segments.visual_notes: Show two hands letting go of each other.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express sudden understanding and comment on someone's carelessness.

narration.subtitle_en: Lin Xuemei finally understands. 'Oh, so that's what happened. This Tianming, how can he be so careless! What happened later?' The phrase [zh:原来是这样] expresses a sudden realization. She then comments on Tianming's character, calling him [zh:马虎], or 'careless'. Finally, she asks [zh:后来呢?], 'What happened later?', to keep the story going.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 原来是这样。这个天明,怎么这么马虎!后来呢?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yuánlái shì zhèyàng. Zhè ge Tiānmíng, zěnme zhème mǎhu! Hòulái ne?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Oh, so that's what happened. This Tianming, how can he be so careless! What happened later?

grammar_points.explanation_en: Used at the beginning of a clause to convey a sudden realization, 'it turns out that...'

segments.visual_notes: Show a lightbulb turning on above Lin Xuemei's head for '原来'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to describe someone's apology and good attitude, and how this reveals their feelings.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa continues explaining what happened. She says, 'Later when he saw me, he kept apologizing, saying he was sorry, and his attitude was especially good.' Let's break this down. [zh:后来] means 'later'. [zh:不停地] means 'incessantly' or 'non-stop'. The key verb is [zh:道歉], 'to apologize'. Finally, [zh:态度特别好] means his 'attitude was especially good'. This part of the sentence describes how sincerely he seemed to be apologizing.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 后来他见到我,不停地给我道歉,说对不起,态度特别好。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hòulái tā jiàn dào wǒ, bùtíng de gěi wǒ dàoqiàn, shuō duìbùqǐ, tàidù tèbié hǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Later when he saw me, he kept apologizing to me, saying he was sorry. His attitude was especially good.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb-object compound meaning to express regret or say sorry. It's more formal than just saying 'duìbùqǐ'.
Refers to one's manner, disposition, or feeling with regard to a person or thing.

segments.visual_notes: Animate an icon for 'apology' (e.g., a person bowing slightly) next to the word 道歉. Highlight '不停地' with a looping arrow animation.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the phrase 'xīnlǐ yǒu wǒ' and understand how to express 'it makes me feel...'

narration.subtitle_en: This is the conclusion Lisa draws from his apology. The sentence means, 'This made me feel that I am still in his heart.' The structure [zh:这让我觉得] is very useful; it means 'This makes me feel...' or 'This leads me to believe...'. The most important phrase here is [zh:心里还是有我]. [zh:心里] means 'in the heart' or 'in one's mind'. [zh:有我] literally means 'has me'. So, the whole phrase conveys the idea that 'he still cares for me' or 'I still have a place in his heart'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这让我觉得他心里还是有我。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhè ràng wǒ juéde tā xīnli háishi yǒu wǒ.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: This made me feel that I am still in his heart.
on_screen_text.notes: The structure '让 (ràng) + someone + feel/think' is very common.

highlight_words.translation: to let, to make (someone do something)
highlight_words.explanation_en: A pivotal verb that indicates one thing causing a certain action or feeling in someone else. It's interchangeable with 'jiào' in this context.
A common word combining 'xīn' (heart) and 'lǐ' (inside) to refer to one's innermost thoughts and feelings.

segments.visual_notes: Show a heart icon with a person's silhouette inside to visualize '心里有我'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to comfort someone and differentiate between two concepts using 'gēn...bùtóng'.

narration.subtitle_en: Xuemei responds to Lisa comfortingly. She starts with [zh:是啊], a casual way to agree, like 'Yeah'. Then she says [zh:别生气了], 'Don't be angry anymore'. The core of her argument is: 'Being careless is completely different from not having you in his heart.' Here, [zh:马虎] means 'careless'. The structure 'A [zh:跟] B [zh:根本不同]' means 'A is fundamentally different from B'. [zh:根本] acts as an adverb meaning 'at all' or 'fundamentally'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 是啊,别生气了!马虎跟心里没有你根本不同。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shì a, bié shēngqì le! Mǎhu gēn xīnlǐ méiyǒu nǐ gēnběn bùtóng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Yeah, don't be angry! Being careless is completely different from not having you in his heart.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adjective describing someone who is forgetful or not meticulous. It literally combines 'horse' and 'tiger'.
Used as an adverb, often in negative sentences, to emphasize the complete or fundamental nature of something.
Means 'not the same'. It's formed by 'bù' (not) and 'tóng' (same).

segments.visual_notes: Visually separate '马虎' and '心里没有你' into two boxes, with a large '≠' symbol between them to emphasize '根本不同'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand hypothetical sentences with 'rúguǒ' and the meaning of 'shuōbudìng'.

narration.subtitle_en: Xuemei continues with a hypothetical warning, leaving the sentence unfinished for dramatic effect. She says, 'If one day you two stop fighting, maybe you will...' Let's look at the words. [zh:如果] means 'if'. [zh:有一天] means 'one day'. [zh:不吵了] means 'stop fighting'. The key word is [zh:说不定], which means 'perhaps' or 'maybe'. The sentence trails off, implying that if they stop fighting, it might mean they've given up on the relationship and will break up.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 如果有一天不吵了,说不定你们就……
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Rúguǒ yǒu yì tiān bù chǎo le, shuōbudìng nǐmen jiù...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: If one day you two stop fighting, maybe you will...
on_screen_text.notes: The ellipsis (...) indicates an unfinished thought, implying a potential breakup.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to introduce a conditional or hypothetical clause.
An adverb expressing uncertainty or possibility. It literally means 'can't say for sure'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the ellipsis '...' to pulse or fade in and out to emphasize the unfinished, ominous nature of the sentence.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn idioms for forgetfulness and how to say two people are similar.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa finishes Xuemei's thought: '[you] really will break up.' Then she compares her own boyfriend, Ke Lin, to Tianming. [zh:实际上] means 'actually' or 'in fact'. The phrase [zh:跟...差不多] means 'is similar to...' or 'is more or less the same as...'. She describes them as [zh:常常忘这忘那、丢三拉四的]. These are two fantastic idioms for describing someone who is forgetful and scatterbrained. We'll look at them more closely in a moment.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 真的会分手。实际上,柯林跟天明差不多,常常忘这忘那、丢三拉四的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhēnde huì fēnshǒu. Shíjìshang, Kē Lín gēn Tiānmíng chàbuduō, chángcháng wàng zhè wàng nà, diū sān là sì de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: ...really break up. Actually, Ke Lin is not much different from Tianming, always forgetting this and that, very scatterbrained.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb-object phrase literally meaning 'to separate hands', used for couples ending a relationship.
Used to introduce a statement that clarifies or contrasts with a previous idea.
A very common and versatile word. Here it means 'similar' or 'about the same'.

segments.visual_notes: Show two character icons labeled '柯林' and '天明' with a '≈' symbol between them to represent '差不多'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn and understand two common four-character idioms related to being forgetful.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's focus on the two idioms Lisa used. The first is [zh:忘这忘那]. It literally means 'forget this, forget that', and it perfectly describes someone who is always forgetting things. The second is [zh:丢三拉四]. This is a set phrase meaning 'scatterbrained' or 'always losing things'. The numbers three and four here don't have a literal meaning; they are just part of the fixed expression. Both are great for describing a forgetful person in a vivid way.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 忘这忘那 / 丢三拉四
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: wàng zhè wàng nà / diū sān là sì
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: forget this and that / scatterbrained, forgetful
on_screen_text.notes: These are set phrases (成语, chéngyǔ) with a fixed structure.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A descriptive phrase for being very forgetful. It follows the pattern 'Verb-this-Verb-that'.
A four-character idiom describing someone as disorganized and prone to forgetting or losing things.

segments.visual_notes: Animate question marks popping up around a character's head to illustrate the meaning of these idioms.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the 'yīhuìr... yīhuìr...' pattern to describe a series of events.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa gives specific examples of Ke Lin's forgetfulness using a useful pattern: [zh:一会儿...一会儿...]. This means 'one minute... the next minute...' or 'sometimes... other times...'. She says, '[zh:他一会儿忘了我的生日]', 'One minute he forgets my birthday', '[zh:一会儿找不到钥匙]', 'the next he can't find his keys', '[zh:一会儿又]...', 'and then again...'. This structure is perfect for listing a series of frustrating, repetitive actions.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他一会儿忘了我的生日,一会儿找不到钥匙,一会儿又……
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā yīhuìr wàng le wǒ de shēngrì, yīhuìr zhǎo bu dào yàoshi, yīhuìr yòu...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: One minute he forgets my birthday, the next he can't find his keys, and then again...
on_screen_text.notes: The pattern '一会儿...一会儿...' shows actions happening in succession.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Can mean 'in a moment' or, when used in a pair, 'one moment... the next moment...'.
Literally 'birth day'.
The word for a key to a lock.

segments.visual_notes: Show three panels side-by-side: a cake with a question mark, a hand searching for keys, and then an ellipsis. Highlight '一会儿' in each panel.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express frustration with 'ràng rén zháojí'.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa ends her complaint with this short but expressive sentence. [zh:有的时候] means 'sometimes'. The key phrase is [zh:真让人着急]. [zh:真] means 'really'. We've seen [zh:让人] before, meaning 'it makes people...'. And [zh:着急] means 'anxious', 'worried', or 'frustrated'. So, the whole sentence means 'Sometimes it's really frustrating' or 'Sometimes it really makes one anxious'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 有的时候真让人着急。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yǒu de shíhou zhēn ràng rén zháojí.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Sometimes it's really frustrating.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adjective describing a state of anxiety or worry, often due to being in a hurry or facing a problem.

segments.visual_notes: Show a character with a frustrated expression and a 'steam coming out of ears' animation.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express sudden realization with 'yuánlái' and change topics with 'duìle'.

narration.subtitle_en: Xuemei expresses surprise and then cleverly changes the subject. She starts with [zh:是吗], 'Really?'. Then, [zh:原来柯林也有马虎的时候] means 'So it turns out Ke Lin can be careless too.' The word [zh:原来] is key here; it signals a sudden realization. Then, she uses [zh:对了], 'By the way', to pivot to a new question: [zh:你交男朋友的事儿,家里知道吗], 'Does your family know about you having a boyfriend?' This is a very natural way to shift a conversation in Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 是吗?原来柯林也有马虎的时候。对了,你交男朋友的事儿,家里知道吗?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shì ma? Yuánlái Kē Lín yě yǒu mǎhu de shíhou. Duìle, nǐ jiāo nánpéngyou de shìr, jiālǐ zhīdào ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Really? So Ke Lin can be careless too. By the way, does your family know that you have a boyfriend?

highlight_words.translation: so, it turns out that; original
highlight_words.explanation_en: As an adverb, it indicates a sudden discovery of the truth. It's like saying 'Oh, so that's how it is!'
A conversational filler used to introduce a new, often unrelated, topic, as if one has just remembered something.
The verb 'jiāo' means 'to make friends', so 'jiāo nánpéngyou' means to get a boyfriend or be in a relationship.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a lightbulb turning on above the character's head for '原来'. Then, show a speech bubble changing to a new topic for '对了'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to express hesitation and talk about things one hasn't done yet.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa's response shows hesitation. She starts with [zh:这个吗], which is a filler phrase like 'Well...' or 'About that...'. The long pause with the ellipsis also indicates she is reluctant to answer. Her main sentence is [zh:我还没把我跟柯林的事儿告诉家里呢]. The structure [zh:还没...呢] means 'haven't yet...'. The [zh:把] structure is used here to bring the object, 'the matter of Ke Lin and me', to the front. The verb is [zh:告诉], 'to tell'. So, she's saying, 'I haven't told my family about Ke Lin and me yet.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这个吗………………我还没把我跟柯林的事儿告诉家里呢。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhège ma... wǒ hái méi bǎ wǒ gēn Kē Lín de shìr gàosu jiālǐ ne.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Well... I haven't told my family about Ke Lin and me yet.
on_screen_text.notes: '还没...呢' (hái méi...ne) is a common pattern for 'not yet'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common verb used to mean 'to inform' or 'to tell' someone something.

segments.visual_notes: Show a character with a hesitant expression. Animate a zipper closing over her mouth to represent the secret.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach students how to use the structure '(在)...上' to specify the scope or aspect of a comment, equivalent to 'in terms of...' in English.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's begin our grammar deep dive. Our first pattern is used to talk about something 'in terms of' a specific aspect. The structure is [zh:在] plus an abstract noun, followed by [zh:上]. We saw this in the dialogue when describing Lisa and Tianming: [zh:在兴趣上，丽莎跟天明不太一样]. This means 'In terms of interests, Lisa and Tianming are not quite the same.' This structure is very useful for focusing your comments. For example, you could say [zh:在学习上] for 'in terms of studies,' or [zh:在生活上] for 'in terms of daily life.' It helps you be more precise in your descriptions.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 在兴趣上, 丽莎跟天明不太一样。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zài xìngqù shàng, Lìshā gēn Tiānmíng bú tài yíyàng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: In terms of interests, Lisha and Tianming are not really the same.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Used with abstract nouns to mean 'in terms of...' or 'with regard to...'. The initial '在' is optional.

grammar_points.pattern: 在学习上, 他有很多问题。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Zài xuéxí shàng, tā yǒu hěn duō wèntí.
grammar_points.explanation_en: In terms of studies, he has many problems.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the structure '(在) + [Noun] + 上' being assembled. Highlight '兴趣' as the abstract noun.

segments.segment_title: Structure: Verb 来 Verb 去
segments.teaching_goal: To teach the 'Verb 来 Verb 去' construction, which indicates a repetitive or back-and-forth action.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, let's look at a pattern for describing repetitive actions: 'Verb [zh:来] Verb [zh:去]'. This literally means 'verb come, verb go,' and it creates the feeling of an action being done over and over. In the text, Xuemei couldn't figure out what was wrong, so she [zh:想来想去], meaning she 'thought it over and over.' This is a dynamic way to express repetition. You can apply it to other verbs too, like [zh:走来走去] for 'walking back and forth,' or [zh:说来说去] for 'saying something again and again.' It's a vivid and common expression.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 林雪梅想来想去, 想不出他们到底发生了什么事儿。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lín Xuěméi xiǎng lái xiǎng qù, xiǎng bu chū tāmen dàodǐ fāshēng le shénme shìr.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Lin Xuemei thought it over and over, but couldn't figure out what on earth had happened between them.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Signifies a repetitive action, like doing something 'over and over' or 'back and forth'.

grammar_points.pattern: 妈妈叫我学经济, 我想来想去还是选了化学。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Māma jiào wǒ xué jīngjì, wǒ xiǎng lái xiǎng qù háishi xuǎn le huàxué.
grammar_points.explanation_en: My mom wanted me to study economics. After thinking it over and over, I still chose chemistry.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of a character thinking, with the words '想来想去' looping around their head.

segments.segment_title: Comparing 的, 得, and 地
segments.teaching_goal: To clarify the distinct functions of the three structural particles 'de' (的, 得, 地).

narration.subtitle_en: Now for a crucial grammar point: the three 'de' particles. They sound the same but are written differently and have different jobs. First, [zh:的] is used before a noun to link it with a description, like an adjective. For example, [zh:他们的兴趣爱好], 'their interests and hobbies.' Second, [zh:得] comes after a verb or adjective to introduce a complement that describes the action or state. We saw this in [zh:忘得一干二净], 'forgot completely.' Finally, [zh:地] is placed after an adverbial to modify a verb. For instance, [zh:很高兴地答应了], 'happily agreed.' Mastering these three particles will make your Chinese much more accurate.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 的: 他们的兴趣爱好 / 得: 忘得一干二净 / 地: 很高兴地答应了
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: de: tāmen de xìngqù àihào / de: wàng de yì gān èr jìng / de: hěn gāoxìng de dāying le
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: 的: their interests and hobbies / 得: forgot completely / 地: happily agreed
on_screen_text.notes: 的 + Noun | Verb/Adj. + 得 + Complement | Adverbial + 地 + Verb

grammar_points.explanation_en: Links a description to a noun.
Links a verb or adjective to a descriptive complement.
Links an adverbial modifier to a verb.

segments.visual_notes: Use three columns to visually separate the examples for 的, 得, and 地. Use color coding for each particle.

segments.segment_title: The Two Uses of 原来
segments.teaching_goal: To teach the two main uses of '原来': as a marker of sudden realization ('it turns out') and as an adverb/adjective for 'originally' or 'former'.

narration.subtitle_en: The word [zh:原来] is interesting because it has two distinct meanings. First, it can express a sudden realization, like saying 'Oh, so that's how it is!' or 'It turns out that...'. In the dialogue, when Xuemei understands the situation, she says, [zh:原来是这样]. The second meaning is 'originally' or 'in the past.' For this use, it can be an adjective, like [zh:原来的宿舍], 'the original dorm,' or an adverb, like [zh:他原来住在这里], 'He originally lived here.' Context is key to knowing which [zh:原来] you're hearing.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 1. 原来是这样。 (Realization) / 2. 他原来住在学校。 (Originally)
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: 1. Yuánlái shì zhèyàng. / 2. Tā yuánlái zhù zài xuéxiào.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: 1. Oh, so that's what happened. / 2. He originally lived at school.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Expresses a sudden realization: 'It turns out that...'
As an adverb meaning 'originally' or 'in the past'.
As an adjective meaning 'original' or 'former'.

segments.visual_notes: Use a split screen. On one side, a lightbulb icon for the 'realization' meaning. On the other, a calendar/clock icon for the 'originally' meaning.

segments.teaching_goal: To introduce common four-character set phrases (chengyu) and explain the meaning and usage of '一干二净' and '丢三拉四'.

narration.subtitle_en: Chinese is rich with set phrases, often four characters long, called 'chengyu'. Their meanings are not always literal. This lesson has two great examples. First, [zh:一干二净]. It means 'completely' or 'thoroughly,' often used with verbs like 'forget' or 'clean.' Lisa says Tianming [zh:忘得一干二净], 'forgot completely.' The second one is [zh:丢三拉四]. This idiom describes someone who is 'scatterbrained' or 'forgetful.' Lisa uses it to describe both Tianming and Ke Lin. Learning these idioms will make your Chinese sound much more natural.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 一干二净 (thoroughly) / 丢三拉四 (scatterbrained)
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yì gān èr jìng / diū sān lā sì
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: completely, spotless / forgetful, absent-minded

grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: bǎ... wàng de yì gān èr jìng
grammar_points.explanation_en: To forget something completely.

grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Tā chángcháng diū sān lā sì de.
grammar_points.explanation_en: He is often scatterbrained.

segments.visual_notes: Show the idiom '一干二净' wiping a slate clean. For '丢三拉四', show a character dropping items like keys and a phone.

segments.teaching_goal: To review and reinforce the key grammar patterns and phrases from the lesson.

narration.subtitle_en: Great job today! We've covered some very important grammar. We learned how to talk about things 'in terms of' an aspect with [zh:在...上], how to describe repetitive actions with 'Verb [zh:来] Verb [zh:去]', and the two uses of [zh:原来]. We also practiced two common idioms. Review these key phrases on the screen and try to make your own sentences!

on_screen_text.focus_text: Key Phrases Review

segments.visual_notes: Display the key phrases as a list on the screen for final review.
